Phase: Phase 2 — Data Dictionary and Schema Mapping
Issue: Whether to integrate the dataset dictionary with AdPilot schemas before AdPilot source files are available.
Options presented: A: provisional mapping; B: pause for project files; C: create standalone dictionary and postpone schema mapping.
Reason for the selection: The user does not want AdPilot integration at this stage.
Implementation performed: Created a standalone data dictionary and postponed all AdPilot schema mapping.
Reversibility: Fully reversible; documentation and scope decision only.
Notes: AdPilot integration will resume only when explicitly requested.

Phase: Phase 2 — Data Dictionary and Schema Mapping
Issue: How to document and name the source `city` field, which mixes cities, governorates, coastal markets, and broad regions.
Options presented: A: use `market_region`; B: retain `city`; C: use `governorate`.
Reason for the selection: `market_region` is neutral and accurately accommodates mixed geographic levels without claiming formal administrative precision.
Implementation performed: Updated the proposed standardized name and business definition in the standalone data dictionary. The raw `city` column was not renamed or modified.
Notes: A formal city/governorate mapping will require a later approved external mapping.

Phase: Phase 2 — Data Dictionary and Schema Mapping
Issue: How to document the source `subdistrict` field, which mixes compounds, projects, compound groups, neighborhoods, and roads.
Options presented: A: use `submarket_or_compound`; B: use `compound_name`; C: retain `subdistrict`.
Reason for the selection: The neutral name reflects the mixed entity types without falsely treating every value as a formal compound.
Number of affected records: 32,954 non-missing documentation references; 0 data values changed
Implementation performed: Updated the proposed name and definition in the standalone data dictionary. The raw field was not renamed or transformed.
Notes: 6,759 values are missing. Entity classification will require a later approved mapping process.

Issue: Whether to approve the standalone data dictionary and begin non-destructive Phase 3 data-quality analysis.
Options presented: A: approve and begin Phase 3; B: review dictionary; C: revise selected definitions.
Reason for the selection: User approved progressing to non-destructive data-quality analysis.
Implementation performed: Completed a non-destructive Phase 3 diagnostic scan and created issue summaries. No cleaning was applied.
Notes: Each consequential cleaning action will require a separate decision.

Issue: How to handle the single non-property project_recommendation placeholder row.
Options presented: A: exclude from analysis and preserve in audit; B: keep in analytical dataset; C: keep only for listing-type counts.
Reason for the selection: The row is not a property listing and has no usable analytical fields.
Number of affected records: 1 excluded from analytical dataset (0.0025%); preserved in audit
Implementation performed: Created a new processed dataset without the row and saved the full excluded row with exclusion metadata in the audit dataset. Raw data untouched.
Reversibility: Fully reversible using the raw dataset and audit record.

Issue: How to handle 3,918 rows in broad probable-duplicate groups.
Options presented: A: keep all and group later; B: remove 2,321 broad-rule extras; C: run stricter text, agent, contact and reference checks before deciding.
Reason for the selection: Broad exact property signatures can represent distinct units; stricter evidence is needed before removal.
Implementation performed: Ran exact-title/description and graded text-similarity analysis within candidate blocks; exported group and row audit tables.
Notes: Exact-text rule identifies 938 potential extras; strong rule identifies 1,320.

Issue: Which duplicate rule to apply after stricter similarity analysis.
Options presented: A: remove 938 exact-text extras; B: remove 1,320 strong-similarity extras; C: keep all and assign group IDs.
Reason for the selection: Exact normalized title and description matches are the lowest-risk automatic repost rule.
Implementation performed: Retained one canonical listing per exact-text component using latest listed date, then completeness, image count, scrape time, and stable source order. Removed rows were preserved in duplicate and combined exclusion audits.
Reversibility: Fully reversible using the prior processed dataset and audit files.
